response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with ~18 years of experience, current staff-level work, and recent exposure to MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling, but the role requires 8+ years building production AI-driven solutions, which he does not demonstrate. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, data pipelines for ML, or multi-agent frameworks beyond roughly one year of adjacent AI-infrastructure work. His cloud experience (AWS CDK) and TypeScript depth are solid, and his teaching/workshop history suggests customer-facing ability, but the core GenAI expertise gap is too large for a staff-level forward deployed GenAI role.
strengths: 18+ years of broad production engineering across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Recent hands-on work with MCP servers and agentic-workflow developer tooling
Proven communicator: workshops, conference talks, and multi-day courses indicate stakeholder-facing skills
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~1 year of AI-adjacent work)
No demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or multi-agent frameworks
Limited evidence of designing data pipelines or AI systems at enterprise scale on cloud platforms

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and TypeScript/cloud foundation
